summary:
The predominant source changes over time; initially derived mostly from maternal serum until about week ten when fetal urine becomes the major contributor up till delivery. Fetal kidney function matures sufficiently after approximately two-and-a-half months enabling significant urinary output crucially supporting later-stage amniotic fluid levels necessary throughout full-term development without relying heavily upon alternative routes or mechanisms earlier during embryogenesis. Hence, \(\boxed{\text{fetal urination}}\) stands out prominently among options presented hereafter considering both temporal dynamics alongside physiological milestones achieved sequentially within intrauterine environment regulation requirements. To directly address your request succinctly yet comprehensively based strictly onto core aspects pertinent towards answering posed query regarding 'main' origin/source responsible primarily across entire duration unless otherwise specified explicitly :

Amniotic fluid transitioned fundamentally starting mid-pregnancy where previously shared roles between mother & fetus shifted decisively into almost exclusive dependence on developing baby's growing
